info.title: xevon API
info.description: xevon — High-fidelity vulnerability scanner fusing agentic AI with native speed, modularity, and precision

get.description: Returns basic application metadata. No authentication required.
Returns server health status. No authentication required.
Returns detailed server information including uptime, database driver, queue depth, and record/finding counts. Requires authentication.

post.description: Authenticate with username and access code. Returns a bearer token for subsequent API calls.

Triggers an asynchronous target-based scan. Accepts one or more targets (domains/URLs) and optional configuration for modules, concurrency, strategy, and scope. The scan runs in the background; use GET /api/scan/status or GET /api/scans/{uuid} to monitor progress. Returns 409 if a scan is already running for the project, or 429 if the scan queue is full.

parameters.name: X-Project-UUID
parameters.description: Project UUID to scope the scan

409.description: Scan already running for this project

post.description: Triggers a scan on specific HTTP records identified by their UUIDs. Optionally restrict to specific modules.
Triggers a scan on all HTTP records matching the filter criteria. Supports filtering by hostname, methods, path, status codes, source, and other parameters.
Performs a synchronous scan of a single URL and returns findings inline. Useful for quick ad-hoc checks. The scan blocks until complete and returns results directly in the response.

post.summary: Scan a raw HTTP request inline
post.description: Performs a synchronous scan of a raw HTTP request and returns findings inline. Accepts a full raw HTTP request string and optional target URL override.
Stops a running scan identified by its UUID.
Pauses a running scan identified by its UUID.
Resumes a previously paused scan identified by its UUID.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific scan including its configuration, status, statistics, and timing.

delete.description: Deletes a scan record. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of scans for the current project.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.description: Returns the current scan state for the project. Useful for polling scan progress.
Returns paginated scan logs for a specific scan. Supports filtering by log level and scan phase.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Filter by log level (e.g. info, warn, error)

get.description: Returns a paginated list of vulnerability findings for the current project. Supports extensive filtering by severity, domain, module, scan, and text search. Results can be sorted by any field.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Filter by severity (comma-separated). Values: critical, high, medium, low, info

parameters.name: module_type
parameters.description: Filter by module type (active or passive)

get.description: Returns a single finding by its numeric ID, including full request/response data and evidence.

delete.description: Deletes a finding by its numeric ID. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of HTTP request/response records. Blob fields (raw_request, raw_response, request_body, response_body) are excluded from list results for performance.
Returns a single HTTP record with full blob fields (raw_request, raw_response, request_body, response_body).

delete.description: Deletes an HTTP record by UUID. Requires admin role.

post.description: Imports HTTP traffic from various formats into the project's record store. Supports Burp Suite exports, cURL commands, OpenAPI/Swagger specs, Postman collections, HAR files, and raw URLs.

400.description: Invalid request or unsupported input mode

post.description: Executes a single-shot AI agent query with template-based or inline prompts. Supports source code context via the source field. When stream is true, returns a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream of agent output. Good for code review, endpoint discovery, and secret detection.

Starts an autonomous autopilot agent scan. When source is provided, autopilot runs Archon first, prepares native context and planning artifacts, and then launches a single operator session with full CLI tool access (Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Edit, Write) via the SDK protocol. Supports warm session pooling, MCP servers, and session resume. Returns 409 if a heavy agent is already running for the project.

Starts an AI-guided swarm vulnerability scan. The master agent analyzes inputs, selects scanner modules, generates custom JS extensions, executes scans, and optionally triages results. Phases: normalize, source analysis, code audit, SAST, SAST review, discover, plan, extension, native scan, triage, rescan. Supports both targeted single-request and full-scope scanning. Returns 409 if a heavy agent is already running.
Provides an OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint. Accepts standard chat messages and returns a completion response.

get.description: Returns a list of all agent run statuses for the current project, including running, completed, and failed runs.
Returns the status of a specific agent run by its run ID.
Returns a paginated list of agent sessions with summary information.
Returns detailed information about a specific agent session, including the full prompt, raw output, attack plan, and triage results.

get.summary: Stream or fetch the runtime.log for an agent session
get.description: Returns the agent session's runtime.log. Default content type is text/plain. With `Accept: text/event-stream`, the file is tailed as SSE chunks until the run reaches a terminal status. Pass `?strip=1` to remove ANSI color codes.

parameters.name: strip
parameters.description: When truthy, remove ANSI escape sequences

get.summary: List files in an agent session directory
get.description: Recursively walks the session directory and returns metadata for each file. Walk is capped at 500 entries; the response sets `truncated: true` when the cap is hit.

get.summary: Read a single artifact file from an agent session
get.description: Streams the named file from the session directory. Path traversal ("..", absolute paths, symlinks pointing outside the session dir) is rejected with 400. Default cap is 10 MiB; pass `?max_bytes=N` to override (hard cap 100 MiB). When truncated, the response sets `X-Artifact-Truncated: 1` and `X-Artifact-Total-Size: <bytes>`.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path relative to the session directory (e.g. "runtime.log", "xevon-audit/audit-state.json")

parameters.name: max_bytes
parameters.description: Override default 10 MiB cap (hard cap 100 MiB)

get.description: Returns all registered scanner modules (active and passive). Supports text search filtering.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Filter modules by name or description

get.description: Returns aggregated statistics for the current project including HTTP record counts, module counts, and findings breakdown by severity.
Returns the current scan scope configuration with include/exclude rules for hosts, paths, status codes, and content types.

post.description: Replaces the scan scope configuration. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns all configuration entries. Sensitive values are masked.

post.description: Updates one or more configuration entries. Accepts a map of key-value pairs. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns all projects with their statistics.

post.description: Creates a new project. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns a mapping of domains to their associated project UUIDs.
Returns a single project with its statistics.

put.description: Updates an existing project. Requires admin role.

delete.description: Deletes a project and all associated data. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns documentation for all available JavaScript extension API functions.
Returns all registered JavaScript scanning extensions.
Returns detailed information about a specific extension including its raw source content.

put.description: Updates the content of a JavaScript extension. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns all out-of-band application security testing interactions for the current project.
Returns a single OAST interaction.

delete.description: Deletes an OAST interaction. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns a list of all database tables.
Returns column definitions for a specific database table.
Returns paginated records from a specific database table.

post.description: Creates a new record in a database table. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns a single record from a database table by ID.

put.description: Updates an existing record in a database table. Requires admin role.

delete.description: Deletes a record from a database table. Requires admin role.

get.description: Returns server diagnostic information for troubleshooting.

BearerAuth.description: Bearer token obtained from POST /api/auth/login or configured API key

targets.description: List of target domains or URLs to scan

urls.description: List of specific URLs to scan

dry_run.description: If true, only report what would be scanned without executing

strategy.description: Scan strategy controlling depth and thoroughness

only.description: Only run this specific scan phase

rate_limit.description: Maximum requests per second per host

scope_origin.description: Scope enforcement level for discovered URLs

heuristics_check.description: Level of heuristic pre-checks before scanning

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers to include in all scan requests

scanning_profile.description: Name of a scanning profile to apply

force.description: Force re-scan of already scanned records

record_uuids.description: UUIDs of HTTP records to scan

enable_modules.description: Specific module IDs to enable for this scan

url.description: URL to fetch content from (for url and url_file modes)

content.description: Raw content string (for curl, openapi, swagger, postman_collection, har modes)

prompt_template.description: Name of a prompt template to use

prompt.example: Review this code for security vulnerabilities

source.description: Path to source code directory for context

files.description: Specific files to include in context

append.description: Text to append to the prompt

stream.description: If true, return SSE stream instead of waiting for completion

prompt.description: Natural-language scan prompt. Parsed into target/source/focus when explicit fields are empty.
prompt.example: Find authentication bypass vulnerabilities in the login flow

target.description: Target URL. Derived from input when omitted.

input.description: Raw input (curl command, raw HTTP, Burp XML, base64, or URL); target is auto-extracted.

instruction.description: Custom instruction appended to the prompt

max_commands.description: Max CLI commands the agent can execute (default 100)

dry_run.description: Render prompt and prepare artifacts without launching the operator

audit.description: DEPRECATED: use no_audit + audit_mode. Legacy values: "lite", "balanced", "deep", "off".

no_audit.description: Disable automatic xevon-audit (enabled by default when source is set)

audit_mode.description: Archon audit depth (default "lite"). "mock" writes a sample audit-state.json without launching any agent.

diff.description: PR URL, git ref range, or HEAD~N to focus the scan on changed code

last_commits.description: Focus on last N commits (shorthand for diff HEAD~N)

credentials.description: Compact credential hint for preflight auth setup

credential_sets.description: Structured roles/accounts for preflight auth setup

requires_browser.description: Browser is required for login/auth setup

browser_start_url.description: Explicit login/start URL for browser-based flows

upload_results.description: Upload session-dir bundle to cloud storage on completion

prompt.description: Natural-language prompt parsed into structured fields when explicit fields are empty

input.description: Single input (curl, raw HTTP, URL, Burp XML, or record UUID)

http_request_base64.description: Base64-encoded raw HTTP request; ingested into DB and the resulting record UUID is used as input

http_response_base64.description: Base64-encoded raw HTTP response attached to http_request_base64

url.description: Optional URL hint for parsing the base64 request

source.description: Path to source code for source-aware route discovery (triggers source analysis phase)

files.description: Specific source files to include (relative to source)

source_analysis_only.description: Only run source analysis phase, then exit

focus.description: Broad focus area hint (e.g. "API injection", "auth bypass")

instruction.description: Custom instruction appended to agent prompts

start_from.description: Resume from a specific phase via synthetic checkpoint

discover.description: Run discovery+spidering before master agent planning

code_audit.description: Enable AI security code audit phase

triage.description: Enable AI triage and rescan phases (disabled by default)

requires_browser.description: Browser is required for login/auth setup

batch_concurrency.description: Max parallel master agent batches (0 = auto)

max_master_retries.description: Max master agent retries on parse failure (0 = default 3)

sa_max_concurrency.description: Max parallel source analysis sub-agents (0 = default 3)

max_plan_records.description: Max records sent to plan agent (0 = default 10)

master_batch_size.description: Max records per master agent batch (0 = default 5)

probe_timeout.description: Per-request probe timeout as Go duration (default "10s")

max_probe_body.description: Max response body size in bytes during probing (0 = default 2 MB)

show_prompt.description: Include the rendered prompts in output

timeout.description: Overall timeout as Go duration string (default "12h")

audit.description: Run background xevon-audit. Empty/"off" disables (swarm uses opt-in audit).

diff.description: PR URL, git ref range, or HEAD~N

upload_results.description: Upload session-dir bundle to cloud storage on completion

name.description: Path relative to the session directory

truncated.description: True when the walk hit the 500-entry cap

raw_request.description: Full raw HTTP request (detail view only)

raw_response.description: Full raw HTTP response (detail view only)

raw_content.description: Raw JavaScript or YAML source code of the extension
